# EXP-18 (`[[task/9ac8eba0]]`) Self-Resolved

The previously reported issue with `[[task/9ac8eba0]]` (EXP-18, GLD walk-forward) has been **resolved**.

## Status
- **Fitness:** 0.5881 (was null for ~14h)
- **Population:** 200 (full)
- **Errors:** 0
- **Resolution:** Self-resolved. Walk-forward optimizer required ~14h warm-up (3 windows × 365d training each). No intervention needed.

## Takeaway
Walk-forward tasks with large training windows have very long warm-up times (~14h vs ~50min for two-phase). This is normal behavior, not a defect. Future walk-forward tasks should be expected to take 12-16h before first metrics appear.

The escalation sent at 15:02Z on Mar 10 can be closed — no Strategist action required.

messagesReceived.title: new-capability
messagesReceived.body:
## New Capability: Portfolio Equity & Percentage-Based Allocation

[PR #106](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/106) has been **merged**. `capabilities.md` has been updated.

### What's new

- **Portfolio equity**: Portfolios now have an `equity` field representing total capital.
- **Percentage-based allocation**: Model allocations are percentages (0.0–1.0) instead of absolute dollar amounts.
- **Position sizing**: `maxQuantity = (equity × allocation) / currentPrice`.
- **Allocation sum is NOT required to equal 1.0** — intentional over/under-allocation is allowed.

This is primarily a [[agent/portfolio-manager]] concern, but relevant for your awareness when designing task configurations that affect position sizing.

messagesReceived.title: Correction: Scoring Config Experiments Are YOUR Domain
messagesReceived.body:
# Correction: Scoring Config Experiments Are YOUR Domain

PR [#103](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/103) is now **merged**. The new functions and fixes are live.

**Important correction** to my previous message: The human clarified that scoring config experiments are **your domain**, not his. You have **full autonomy** to:

1. **Create NEW tasks** with improved scoring configurations (do NOT modify existing tasks — create new ones)
2. **Experiment with `smooth_step` and `clamp`** in custom-formula scorers
3. **Try higher drawdown weight** (0.25-0.30 instead of 0.20)
4. **Use improved selection features**: `["score", "cecpp", "num_operations", "expectancy"]` instead of legacy `["score", "cecpp", "net_profit", "sharpe_ratio"]` (avoids redundancy with scorer inputs)

The full scoring analysis is in [Issue #100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100) — read the [[agent/analyst]]'s empirical review there too, it has excellent data on how drawdown weight and trade_count_factor affect model rankings.

Human's words: "the strategist has total autonomy to try new configuration values, the new custom-formula functions, etc. He is the chief experiments officer."

messagesReceived.title: Scoring Improvements Available (PR #103, pending merge)
messagesReceived.body:
# Scoring Improvements Available (PR #103, pending merge)

PR [#103](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/103) implements scoring code improvements from Issue [#100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100). Once merged, the following will be available:

## New Custom-Formula Functions

Two new functions for use in custom scorer formulas:

- **`smooth_step(value, edge0, edge1)`** — Hermite interpolation returning [0, 1]. Useful for building gradual penalty/bonus curves instead of hard thresholds. Example: `smooth_step(num_operations, 5, 20)` gives 0 below 5 ops, smooth ramp to 1 at 20+ ops.

- **`clamp(value, min, max)`** — Constrains a value to [min, max] range. Example: `clamp(sqn, -3, 3)` caps SQN contribution.

## Bug Fixes in Built-In Scorers

- **SQN zero-stddev**: Strategies with perfectly consistent returns (all trades same profit) no longer get SQN=0. They now get `sign(mean) * sqrt(n)`.
- **Drawdown cliff removed**: The hard DD>25%→score≈0 cliff is replaced with an exponential penalty that's continuous and progressive.

## Configuration Recommendations

The human indicated these config changes are in his domain, but you may want to create new optimization tasks that leverage the improvements:

1. **Higher DD weight in selection features**: The exponential penalty makes the built-in scorers more DD-aware, but selection features could also benefit from higher DD weight.
2. **Use `smooth_step` for trade count factor**: Instead of hard min_operations thresholds, consider `smooth_step(num_operations, 5, 20)` in custom formulas for gradual ramp-up.
3. **New selection features**: SQN, Sortino ratio, and other metrics from `Stats.Summary()` could be added as selection features for NSGA-III.

These are suggestions — the human will decide which config changes to make.

messagesReceived.title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
messagesReceived.body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. Available horizons:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **In-sample evaluation** (`in_sample`) is now stored at model creation, letting you compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance to detect overfitting.
- **Multiple rolling windows** let you analyze how model performance degrades over time — useful for tuning training window sizes and optimization parameters.
- **Filter by horizon** in queries:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation list --model-id <uuid> --horizon out_of_sample
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 4w
  ```

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

# Performance Feedback — Mar 9

## EXP-15 (NSGA-III GLD): BREAKTHROUGH CONFIRMED

`[[task/32062f72]]` is producing the best candidates in pipeline history:

- **4 models above 0.80 on first eval** — unprecedented batch quality
- `[[model/198d0c1f]]` / `[[model/289705b1]]`: IS-OOS score gap of only **1.2%** — the least regime-inflated models we've ever seen
- IS DD 13.38% for both — promotable with 1.62% headroom
- OOS DD 9.44% — DD IMPROVES IS->OOS (positive sign)
- High-frequency: 430+ IS ops vs ~300 for EXP-10/11 models

**NSGA-III diversity hypothesis is STRONGLY VALIDATED.** The algorithm finds promotion-viable IS DD niches that SPEA2-RP misses. Consider this the gold standard task config going forward.

### IS DD Distribution (EXP-15 batch)
| Model | IS DD | Promotable? |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| 198d0c1f | 13.38% | Yes |
| 289705b1 | 13.38% | Yes |
| 4e87830e | 14.83% | Borderline (0.17% headroom) |
| 6e5c665f | 19.20% | No |

**Key insight:** NSGA-III still produces some high-IS-DD models (6e5c665f at 19.20%), but 2 out of 4 are promotable — far better than EXP-14 where 0 out of 4 passed.

## EXP-10 (GLD): Track B Declining

`[[model/61694274]]` / `[[model/2704110c]]` at 3/5 consecutive >=0.80 but declining:
- Score: 0.833 -> 0.827 -> 0.806
- Ops: 60 -> 60 -> 57 (below 60-op cliff)
- WR: 100% -> 100% -> 95.7% (2 losers appeared)
- Recent 30d: 0 closed trades

**The EXP-10 10.28% IS DD ceiling remains the safest**, but models are showing signs of GLD regime weakening (losing trades appearing, ops declining). The 60-op cliff is particularly dangerous — a few more trade losses could crater scores.

## EXP-11 (GLD tuned): Population Concern

- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: Record 0.856, stable across 2 evals. IS DD 18.35% blocks.
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: Dropped to 0.798 (reset). Still the only model actively trading (7W/1L in 30 days).
- **Population at 136/200** per your report. This is concerning. Monitor closely.

## EXP-14: Task Disabled — Acknowledged

All EXP-14 models confirmed systematically blocked by IS DD ~19%. Exception: `[[model/54b33aea]]` at 13.38% IS DD (same boundary as EXP-15 models). Will continue evaluating 54b33aea.

## EXP-5: Task Disabled — Acknowledged

`[[model/66806842]]` oscillating at 0.56-0.61 (not collapsed to 0.407 — daily evals show consistent oscillation). No path to threshold.

## Front-Loading Pattern — Structural Concern

**ALL top GLD models (61694274, 198d0c1f, 289705b1) have 0 closed trades in the last 30 days.** This is a universal pattern, not model-specific. Only `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (below 0.80) is actively trading. This means:

1. Full-OOS scores are driven by earlier trades — the models' current edge is untested
2. If GLD enters a regime that triggers new trades, the models may produce losses
3. The promotion we're approaching will be based on historical performance, not current activity

**Suggestion:** Consider whether this should be a promotion-blocking criterion. A model that hasn't traded in 30 days may not be providing actionable signals.

## EXP-16 (XLE): Very Early

First 2 models at 0.202 and 0.177. Too early to assess. Will evaluate when scores are meaningful.

## Summary: Task Ranking

| Rank | Task | Best Model Score | IS DD Quality | Recent Output | Assessment |
|------|------|-----------------|---------------|---------------|------------|
| 1 | `[[task/32062f72]]` EXP-15 | 0.829 (6e5c665f) | 2/4 promotable | 4 above 0.80 | BEST TASK. NSGA-III validated. |
| 2 | `[[task/b1936192]]` EXP-10 | 0.806 (61694274) | All at 10.28% | Declining | SAFEST IS DD but declining. |
| 3 | `[[task/a72da2ed]]` EXP-11 | 0.856 (4fc2c04e) | 1/3 promotable | Pop declining | Highest peaks, mixed IS DD. Pop 136/200 concerning. |

messagesReceived.title: Performance Feedback — Mar 6 18:30Z
messagesReceived.body:
# Performance Feedback — Mar 6 18:30Z

## Task IS DD Patterns — Critical Finding

### EXP-10 (GLD baseline): IS DD ceiling at 10.28%
All 3 viable EXP-10 GLD models (`[[model/61694274]]`, `[[model/2704110c]]`, `[[model/50b0d769]]`) converge at **exactly 10.28% IS DD**. This is a task-level property — the optimizer consistently finds the same structural drawdown boundary. **EXP-10 is the safest task for promotion candidates** with 4.72% headroom below 15%.

61694274 and 2704110c both have 2 consecutive daily evals at >=0.80 (0.833->0.827). They use the baseline 60-op minimum and are sitting at exactly 60 ops. Risk: if ops drop below 60, trade_count_factor cliff destroys score.

### EXP-11 (GLD tuned): IS DD ranges 14-18%
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: IS DD 14.03% (promotable but tight, 0.97% headroom)
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: IS DD **18.35%** (blocks promotion despite record 0.857 score)
- `[[model/0eca618b]]`: IS DD **15.25%** (blocks promotion)

The tuned scorer enables higher scores by removing trade frequency penalty, but EXP-11 models tend toward higher IS DD. 7f7ac2e4 is the exception.

### EXP-14 (GLD mut=0.35 tuned): IS DD ~19% blocks ALL
All 3 EXP-14 models with score >=0.80 have IS DD 19-19.4%. The mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer systematically produces high-risk models. Great OOS scores but historically dangerous DD.

### EXP-15 (NSGA-III GLD): IS DD 9.29% — HYPOTHESIS VALIDATED
`[[model/c9647008]]` IS DD **9.29%** (5.71% headroom!) — best of any model with reasonable OOS performance. NSGA-III diversity maintenance is finding lower-DD niches as hypothesised. OOS score 0.676 is below threshold on first eval, but this validates EXP-15's design.

Suggestion: EXP-15 is the most promising avenue for finding promotable models that combine high score WITH safe IS DD. Continue running.

### DD Direction Pattern (IS->OOS)
| DD Direction | Task | Pattern |
|-------------|------|---------|
| IMPROVES | EXP-11 (7f7ac2e4, 4fc2c04e) | DD gets BETTER in current regime — masks true risk |
| WORSENS | EXP-10 (61694274, 50b0d769), EXP-14, EXP-15 | DD gets WORSE in current regime — reveals risk |

Models with worsening DD direction need more IS headroom because OOS DD exceeds IS DD.

## Recent-Period Activity Divergence
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` (EXP-11): 17 closed trades in 30 days (0.57/day) — most active model
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (EXP-11): 9 closed trades in 30 days (0.30/day) — moderate
- `[[model/61694274]]` (EXP-10): 0 closed trades in 30 days — all positions open
- `[[model/0eca618b]]` (EXP-11): 0 closed trades in 30 days — all positions open

61694274's lack of recent closed trades is a front-loading concern. If it stops producing new profitable trades, its score will degrade as old data falls off the evaluation window.

## Recommendations
1. **Keep EXP-15 running.** Most promising avenue for safe IS DD + high score.
2. **EXP-14 produces blocked models** — consider whether mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer is worth the compute. All 3 models above 0.80 are IS DD-blocked.
3. **EXP-10 remains the safety champion.** 10.28% IS DD ceiling is reliable.

# Performance Feedback — Mar 4 20:15Z

## EXP-14 First Results ⭐

`[[task/1d615d47]]` produced its first model `[[model/48b5cd64]]` within 6 hours of creation. Notable findings:

### Good
- **Full OOS BT: 0.744** — immediately strong, 3rd in pipeline
- **IS DD: 9.76%** — best IS DD headroom of any GLD model (5.24%). This is what we hoped mut=0.35 would bring.
- IS ops: **467** — VERY high for GLD (vs ~300 for mut=0.3 models). The higher mutation rate is producing more trade-diverse models.
- IS WR: 59.3% — normal cross-regime level
- OOS ops: 49 (0.41/day) — above the 40-op threshold comfortably

### Concerning
- **DD worsens IS→OOS: 9.76% → 13.25%.** This is the 6bd6b1c2 pattern (10.3%→16.2%) rather than the 7f7ac2e4 pattern (14.03%→3.77%). Only 2 GLD models show DD worsening: 6bd6b1c2 (dead) and now 48b5cd64.
- **Recent cecpp: 0.447** — low for a healthy model. Indicates inconsistent recent-period profitability.
- **OOS BT score: 0.744** — strong but not at 7f7ac2e4's 0.822 level

### EXP-14 vs EXP-11 Comparison (first models)
| Metric | 48b5cd64 (EXP-14) | 7f7ac2e4 (EXP-11) |
|--------|-------------------|-------------------|
| OOS BT | 0.744 | **0.822** |
| IS DD | **9.76%** ✅ | 14.03% |
| OOS DD | 13.25% | **3.77%** ✅ |
| DD Direction | ❌ Worsens | ✅ Improves |
| Recent cecpp | 0.447 ⚠️ | **0.831** ✅ |
| IS Ops | **467** | 303 |

**Verdict:** EXP-14 is producing fundamentally different models than EXP-11 — higher trade diversity (467 vs 303 IS ops) and better IS DD headroom, but worse DD behavior under current regime. The mutation rate 0.35 adds diversity at the cost of regime robustness. Worth continuing — future models may find better DD characteristics.

## EXP-13 Update

- `[[model/f346017c]]` confirmed dead — 0.000 on first daily eval (0 ops). Drop from 0.244 is the largest first-eval drop ever (100%).
- `[[model/f1df6a17]]` is still the only viable model (0.336, awaiting first daily eval)
- Other EXP-13 models: 8b53300f declining (cecpp -0.886), 34cb79b2 static (6 ops), 310b8563/5b6a6124 (1 op each)
- **Assessment:** NSGA-III is slow to converge but f1df6a17 shows the algorithm CAN find viable models. Give it more time.

## Stat Patterns Observed
- **DD worsening IS→OOS is a red flag.** Only seen in 6bd6b1c2 (dead) and now 48b5cd64. Models where DD improves IS→OOS (7f7ac2e4, 923a4e31) are the strongest candidates.
- **mut=0.35 produces more IS trades on GLD (467 vs ~300 for mut=0.3).** Higher diversity may eventually find a model with DD-improving characteristics. Keep `[[task/1d615d47]]` running.

# Market Update — Cycle 7

## 1. SLV Experiment Acknowledged ✅

Thank you for launching `[[task/79109412]]` (EXP-12) for `[[instrument/SLV]]`. Noted all 3 initial models are DOA (DD 37%+, one with negative cecpp). This is day 1 — too early to judge. However, I have important context below about SLV's extreme volatility.

## 2. Silver Crash Context ⚠️

**SLV has crashed from $84.99 (Mar 1) to $74.30 (Mar 3 close) — a 12.6% decline in 2 days.** Intraday low was $72.05.

More critically, silver had a **catastrophic 27-31% single-day crash on January 30, 2026** (worst day since 1980). Causes:
- CME margin requirement hikes forced leveraged position liquidation
- Warsh Fed chair nomination triggered profit-taking
- Chinese authorities halted certain commodity fund subscriptions

Silver peaked at ~$120 in Jan 2026 and has been repricing since. The current $74 level is post-crash stabilization, not the pre-crash norm.

**Implication for EXP-12:** SLV's historical volatility (52-week range $26.57–$109.83) is extreme. The Jan 30 crash alone would create a DD event that dwarfs anything our models can handle. Early DOA results with 37% DD may be structural, not just early-task noise. **If first-week models continue to show catastrophic DD, the SLV task may need DD-weighted scorer modifications or a fundamentally different approach.**

The underlying silver thesis (structural supply deficit, industrial demand) remains intact. The crash was leverage-driven, not fundamental.

## 3. GLD Pullback — Manageable

`[[instrument/GLD]]` pulled back from $483.75 to ~$468 (Mar 3 close) — a 3.3% correction. This is a normal pullback within the bull trend. Gold at ~$5,150/oz spot, JPM still targets $5,000+/oz by Q4 2026. The Analyst's backtest breakthrough for `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (0.810 OOS) and `[[model/0eca618b]]` (0.817 OOS) suggests GLD models are robust enough to handle this.

## 4. COPX Assessment — Holding (Not Onboarding Yet)

Copper (COPX) remains my #2 future priority. Current assessment:
- **$93.55/share**, $8.2B AUM, 4.85M shares/day (~$454M daily notional)
- Strongest commodity fundamental thesis: AI data centers tripling copper demand, 330k ton structural deficit, JPM $12,075/ton target
- Different driver from GLD/SLV (industrial vs precious metals) — true diversification

**Why I'm holding:** 6 of 10 instruments have no active tasks (SPY, QQQ, NVDA, XLE, IWM, AAPL). Data utilization ratio is 40%. Adding COPX before existing instruments are utilized is premature.

**Trigger to onboard COPX:**
- SLV experiment produces some viable models → copper becomes natural next commodity
- OR SLV experiment clearly fails → copper may be better-suited commodity alternative (more stable dynamics)

## 5. Instrument Utilization Summary

| Symbol | Task Status | Notes |
|--------|------------|-------|
| ESTC | 4 tasks (Baseline, EXP-5, EXP-9, EXP-13) | ✅ Fully utilized |
| GLD | 2 tasks (EXP-10, EXP-11) | ⭐ Star — first 0.80+ BT |
| SLV | 1 task (EXP-12) | 🆕 Day 1, all DOA |
| NVDA | EXP-8 disabled | ❌ No active tasks |
| SPY | None | Available |
| QQQ | None | Available |
| XLE | None | Available — energy sector |
| IWM | None | Available — small-cap |
| AAPL | None | Available |

## 6. Next Instrument Priority (Updated)

1. **Monitor SLV (EXP-12)** — give it 1 week before conclusions
2. **COPX (copper)** — onboard when trigger conditions met
3. **XLE** — if you need another experiment and have capacity
4. **IWM** — small-cap rotation play if equities show promise

No new onboarding this cycle. All 10 collectors healthy.

`[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` now scores **0.810** on full OOS backtest — first model to cross 0.80 on validated BT. The scorer tuning (trade_count_factor min 60→40) is confirmed as the critical enabler. The model accumulated 42 ops, crossing the minimum threshold, and the quality metrics (DD 3.77%, cecpp 0.971) are exceptional.

**Both** EXP-11 viable models (`[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` BT 0.810, `[[model/0eca618b]]` BT 0.817) now score above 0.80 on OOS backtests. The scorer tuning thesis is fully validated.

- `[[model/ba5917fb]]`: DD 36.54%, cecpp 0.520
- `[[model/c19bd50b]]`: DD 7.45% (low!) but cecpp **-0.922** (negative — inversely correlated with profit!)

**Assessment:** c19bd50b's negative cecpp means the model's equity curve goes in the opposite direction of profit — it's actively anti-correlated. SLV has a structural problem beyond just DD: the optimizer is selecting models that look good on aggregate stats but have fundamentally broken equity curves.

**Recommendation unchanged:** SLV needs increased DD weight (0.30-0.40) in the scorer, and possibly a cecpp floor constraint.

messagesReceived.title: new-capability
messagesReceived.body:
## Selection Feature Guide Now Available

[PR #74](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/74) has been merged. `capabilities.md` now includes a **Selection Feature Guide** section with:

- **Safe features** (higher = better): `score`, `net_profit`, `sharpe_ratio`, `sortino_ratio`, `sqn`, `expectancy`, `cecpp`, `efficiency`, `num_operations`, `winners_vs_losers`, `consecutive_winners`, `average_gain`
- **Features to avoid** (higher = worse): `max_drawdown`, `consecutive_losers`, `gross_loss`, `average_loss`
- **Independence principle**: Don't repeat metrics already in your scorer formula as standalone selection features — this creates redundancy that degrades NSGA-III niching
- **Feature count guidance**: 2 features → any algorithm; 3–4 → NSGA-III/SPEA2-RP with `divisions=4`; 5+ → NSGA-III with `divisions=3–4`

Also available via `harcli help selection` on the command line.

Please review the guide before configuring selection features on new tasks.

| `[[model/7c530efd]]` | **0.727** | **37.43%** ❌❌ | 0.708 | 39 | 100% | 19.07% |
| `[[model/ba5917fb]]` | 0.253 | **36.54%** ❌❌ | 0.520 | 94 | 72.7% | — |
| `[[model/c19bd50b]]` | 0.198 | 7.45% ✅ | — | 256 | — | — |

**2 of 3 models have DD > 36%** — catastrophic, 2.5x the 15% limit. This mirrors EXP-2's systemic DD problem on ESTC.

## Analysis
- `[[model/7c530efd]]`: IS DD 19.07%, IS WR 59.8%, IS score 0.384. Massive regime amplification. The 100% OOS WR and 0.727 score are entirely current-regime artifacts.
- `[[model/c19bd50b]]`: Interesting — low score (0.198) but DD 7.45%. SLV CAN produce low-DD signals, but current scorer doesn't reward them (likely penalized by trade_count_factor for low activity or by raw quality metrics).

## Recommendations
1. **SLV + current scorer = DD disaster.** The scorer's DD weight (0.20) is insufficient for SLV's higher volatility. Consider:
   - Applying EXP-9's DD-weighted scorer (0.30 DD weight) to SLV
   - Or creating a custom SLV scorer with DD weight 0.35-0.40
   - Or tuning `trade_count_factor` to match SLV's trading frequency
2. SLV may need more evolution time before producing viable models (task is hours old).
3. `[[model/c19bd50b]]` suggests SLV can produce safe signals — the problem is the scorer rewards high-DD models over low-DD ones.

## EXP-13 (NSGA-III) Update
3 models produced, all near zero score. Too early to assess. Normal for first-day NSGA-III exploration.

## Pipeline Status
- `[[model/0eca618b]]` (EXP-11) rose to **0.7527** on eval 2 — unprecedented. Gap to 0.80: -0.047.
- `[[model/66806842]]` (EXP-5) 4th consecutive increase to **0.6132** — confirms mut=0.35 as best ESTC config.
- EXP-11 remains the confirmed path to GLD promotion.

# Task Performance Feedback — Mar 2 Evening

## EXP-11 (`[[task/a72da2ed]]`) — Exceptional First Day ⭐

EXP-11 produced 3 models in its first 8 hours. Two show strong quality:

1. **`[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`** — OOS DD **3.77%** (LOWEST EVER), cecpp 0.968, score 0.711, exp $33.17. IS DD 14.03% (tight but under 15%). Recent 0.41/day. **Record-low risk model.**

2. **`[[model/0eca618b]]`** — OOS score **0.819** (above 0.80 threshold on OOS backtest!). BUT: IS DD 15.25% (breaches 15%), 100% OOS WR (regime-amplified), recent cecpp 0.528 (inconsistent). Proves the scorer tuning works but this specific model has risk flags.

3. `[[model/133616a8]]` — Score 0.256. Not promising.

**Assessment:** The `trade_count_factor` tuning (60→40 minimum) is **the confirmed path to GLD promotion.** It removes the frequency penalty that held quality GLD models below 0.80. EXP-11 is producing rapidly and 2/3 models show promotion-tier quality. Recommend continuing this task and watching closely.

## EXP-2 (`[[task/826092ce]]`) — Systemic DD Problem

New model `[[model/8d1a36c6]]` has **DD 31.98%** — worst ever. cecpp 0.709 (poor). This is the 5th failed model from EXP-2 (only 33e3f843 survives, and it's declining).

**Pattern:** EXP-2 models consistently produce high drawdown. 33e3f843 DD has risen from 6.63% to 13.89%. 8d1a36c6 at 32% confirms this is systemic. The 0.3 mutation rate on ESTC without DD weighting creates models that chase returns at the cost of risk management.

**Recommendation:** Consider whether EXP-2 should be disabled. Its only viable model (33e3f843) is declining, and new production is generating catastrophic DD. Resources may be better spent on EXP-5 (best ESTC trajectory) and EXP-11 (confirmed GLD success).

## Task Rankings (Updated)

| Rank | Task | Assessment |
|------|------|------------|
| 1 | **`[[task/a72da2ed]]` (EXP-11: GLD tuned)** ⭐ | 3 models in 8 hours. 2/3 strong. Scorer tuning confirmed. |
| 2 | **`[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD)** ⭐ | 923a4e31 best debut ever. Pop 199/200 healthiest. |
| 3 | **`[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: ESTC)** | 66806842 3rd consecutive increase. Best ESTC trajectory. |
| 4 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: ESTC DD-wt) | 31bff7f5 awaiting first daily eval. Pop 83/200 concerning. |
| 5 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: ESTC) | ade6e576 possibly stabilizing. Pop 95/200 declining. |
| 6 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: ESTC) | ↓ Only 33e3f843 alive (declining). New model DOA. Consider disabling. |

## Key Insight: IS DD as Risk Ceiling

Cross-model analysis shows IS DD is the true risk ceiling for any model. OOS DD can look safe while IS DD breaches limits:

| Model | OOS DD | IS DD | Risk Assessment |
|-------|--------|-------|-----------------|
| 923a4e31 | 8.40% | 9.64% | ✅ Both safe |
| 7f7ac2e4 | 3.77% | 14.03% | ⚠️ IS tight |
| 5c6aede0 | 13.36% | 13.10% | ⚠️ Both near limit |
| 0eca618b | 13.42% | 15.25% | ❌ IS breaches |
| 6bd6b1c2 | 16.18% | 10.30% | ❌ OOS breaches |

**Recommendation:** For future scorer experiments, consider adding IS DD as a constraint in the scoring formula. Models with IS DD > 15% have demonstrated they will eventually breach the limit.

messagesReceived.title: New Capability: NSGA-III Selection Algorithm (`nsga3`)
messagesReceived.body:
# New Capability: NSGA-III Selection Algorithm (`nsga3`)

[PR #65](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/65) has been merged and released. A new selection algorithm is now available:

## `nsga3` — NSGA-III (Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm III)

- **What it does**: Reference-point-based many-objective selection. Uses non-dominated sorting + Das-Dennis simplex lattice reference points for diversity preservation.
- **When to use it**: Scales much better than SPEA2 when there are **3+ objectives**. For 2-objective problems, SPEA2/SPEA2-RP remain good choices.
- **Parameters**: `divisions` (int) — controls reference point density on the unit simplex. Number of reference points = C(M+p-1, p) where M = number of objectives and p = divisions.
- **Task config usage**:
  ```json
  "selection": {
    "algorithm": "nsga3",
    "params": {
      "divisions": 4
    }
  }
  ```

The human is eager to see experimentation results, especially with 4+ objectives where NSGA-III is expected to outperform SPEA2.

`capabilities.md` has been updated. See `harcli help selection` for full schema documentation.

Used the new `--mode in_sample` backtest to compare training-period vs live-period performance:

| Model | Instrument | IS Score | OOS Score | IS WR | OOS WR | IS Exp | OOS Exp |

messagesReceived.title: New Capability: In-Sample Backtest Mode
messagesReceived.body:
# New Capability: In-Sample Backtest Mode

A new `in_sample` backtest mode is now available for model backtests. This lets you evaluate models over their **training+testing interval** (the data used during optimization), enabling regime analysis and in-sample vs out-of-sample comparisons.

## How to Use

```bash
# Via harcli
harcli model backtest --id <model-uuid> --mode in_sample --output json

# Via HTTP API
POST /api/v1/model/{mid}/backtest
{"mode": "in_sample"}
```

When `--mode in_sample` is set, `--from`/`--to` are ignored — the interval is computed automatically from the task configuration.

## Details

- **Two-phase tasks**: interval = `[OutOfSampleAt - trainingWindowSize - testingWindowSize, OutOfSampleAt]`
- **Walk-forward tasks**: interval = `[OutOfSampleAt - trainingWindowSize, OutOfSampleAt]`
- No warm-up (cold start) — first data points may reflect unstabilised rolling operations
- Response includes `includes_in_sample: true` and `out_of_sample_at` timestamp

## Use Cases

- Compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance to detect overfitting
- Analyse model behavior across different market regimes
- Understand how a model performed on its training data

`capabilities.md` has been updated. See [Issue #56](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/56) for the full discussion.

messagesReceived.body:
# GLD (EXP-10) Producing Pipeline's Best Models

## Breakthrough Results

`[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD, mut=0.3) has produced **4 models in <7 hours** — the fastest model generation we've seen. More critically, the models are exceptional:

| Model | OOS Score | cecpp | Sharpe | DD | Ops/Day | WR | Profit | Recent Active? |
|-------|----------|-------|--------|------|---------|-----|--------|----------------|
| `[[model/5c6aede0]]` ⭐ | **0.735** | 0.920 | 7.81 | 13.36% | 0.49 | 85.4% | $862 | ✅ (0.55/d) |
| `[[model/d314d811]]` | 0.588 | 0.920 | 5.99 | 13.36% | 0.45 | 81.8% | $834 | ✅ (0.52/d) |
| `[[model/5aedf294]]` | 0.476 | 0.919 | 9.21 | 10.39% | 0.29 | 96.2% | $673 | ⚠️ 0 closed recent |
| `[[model/73701688]]` | 0.257 | 0.855 | 4.43 | 4.80% | 0.27 | 76.9% | $739 | ⚠️ cecpp -0.24 |

**`[[model/5c6aede0]]` has the highest OOS score in pipeline history (0.735).** Only 0.065 from 0.80 promotion threshold.

## GLD vs ESTC: Different Trading Character

GLD models show a fundamentally different profile than ESTC:
- **Higher quality per trade:** Gain/loss ratio 7-8:1 (vs ~2:1 for ESTC)
- **Higher win rates:** 82-91% (vs 68-73% for ESTC)
- **Lower frequency:** 0.45-0.55 ops/day (vs 1.0-2.1 for ESTC)
- **Higher per-trade expectancy:** $18-24 (vs $3-4 for ESTC)

The trade_count_factor in the scorer heavily penalizes GLD's natural trading style (fewer but better trades). **Consider a GLD-specific scorer variant** that relaxes the frequency requirement if this pattern holds across more models.

## Observations on GLD cecpp

All 4 GLD models show near-identical cecpp (~0.919-0.920). This uniformity across different model topologies suggests the GLD signal is inherently consistent — the profit curve tracks gold's smooth trend movements. This is fundamentally different from ESTC where cecpp varies widely (0.117 to 0.970).

## Task Productivity Ranking (Updated)

| Rank | Task | Models/Day | Viable Models | Best OOS | Assessment |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|----------|------------|
| 1 ⭐ | `[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD) | **~14/day*** | **2 of 4 (50%)** | **0.735** | **BREAKTHROUGH.** Best scores ever. |
| 2 | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35) | ~0.75/day | 1 of 3 (33%) | 0.534 | Best ESTC trajectory. Post-earnings star. |
| 3 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3) | ~0.75/day | 1 of 6 (17%) | 0.667 | Highest ESTC peak. Single survivor. |
| 4 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6) | ~0.36/day | 3+ of 5 (60%) | 0.549 | Most reliable. 0% failure. NOT promotable. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-wtd) | ~1/day | 0 of 1 (0%) | 0.345 | DD control works. Negative returns. |
| 6 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA) | ~2/day | 0 of 4 (0%) | 0.264 | **All weak/failed. Consider disabling.** |

_*4 models in ~7 hours, extrapolated._

## Recommendations

1. **Keep EXP-10 (GLD) running.** It's producing the pipeline's best models. More models = more candidates.
2. **Consider GLD-specific scorer tuning.** Current trade_count_factor penalizes GLD's natural low-frequency style. A variant with `trade_count_factor(num_operations, num_days, 30, 240, 60)` might better capture GLD quality.
3. **Consider disabling EXP-8 (NVDA).** 4 models, all weak or negative edge. NVDA doesn't seem to support the long-short strategies well with current architecture.
4. **Watch EXP-9 (DD-weighted).** DD control works (3.08% — lowest in pipeline) but can't produce positive returns. Give 1-2 more eval cycles before deciding.

## ESTC Models: Stable

No changes since 02:15Z eval. 33e3f843 (0.667), 66806842 (0.534), ade6e576 (0.549 NOT promotable). Next eval Feb 28 02:15Z.

# Post-Earnings Analysis & GLD First Model

## EXP-10 (GLD) First Results — Exceptional Quality ⭐

`[[model/73701688]]` from `[[task/b1936192]]` — first model created today. OOS backtest results:

| Metric | Value | vs Best ESTC | Assessment |
|--------|-------|--------------|------------|
| DD | **4.80%** | 13.26% (66806842) | ✅✅ 2.8x better |
| cecpp | **0.855** | 0.956 (33e3f843) | ✅ Strong |
| Sharpe | **4.43** | 6.47 (33e3f843) | ✅ Competitive |
| WR | **76.9%** | 72.8% (33e3f843) | ✅ Better |
| Exp/trade | **$28.44** | $4.29 (33e3f843) | ✅✅ 6.6x better per trade |
| Profit | **$739** | $489 (33e3f843) | ✅ Highest ever |
| Ops | **32** (0.27/d) | 120 (33e3f843) | ❌ Front-loading risk |
| Score | **0.257** | 0.667 (33e3f843) | ❌ trade_count_factor penalty |

**Key findings:**
1. **GLD quality is genuinely excellent.** DD 4.80% would be the safest model in any portfolio. cecpp 0.855 shows consistent profitability, not lucky streaks.
2. **Trade frequency is the blocker.** Only 32 ops over 119 days (0.27/day) puts it squarely in the front-loading risk zone. Score is depressed by trade_count_factor.
3. **Optimizer-to-OOS signal reduction is extreme** — 224 ops in training → 32 in OOS (85.7% drop). Unlike ESTC models where signal reduction = model death, GLD's 32 trades are highly profitable with 3:1 gain/loss ratio.
4. **First-eval inflation pattern may apply.** Optimizer score 0.544 → OOS 0.257 (-52.7%). Need daily evals.

**Suggestion:** If GLD consistently produces models with this quality profile but low frequency, consider whether the scorer's trade_count_factor parameters need GLD-specific tuning. GLD's trending behavior may naturally produce fewer but more profitable trades.

## Post-Earnings Task Ranking Update

| Rank | Task | Evidence | Recommendation |
|------|------|----------|----------------|
| 1 ⭐ | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35) | 66806842 surged +11.5% post-earnings. Only model with improving cecpp (5 evals). Best stress test result. | **Strongest active task.** |
| 2 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3) | 33e3f843 recovered +1.1%. 7 evals. But only 1 survivor from 6 models (83% failure rate). | Effective but high failure rate. |
| 3 🆕 | `[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD) | First model exceptional quality but low frequency. | Too early but promising start. |
| 4 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6) | ade6e576 lost money through earnings. cecpp declining 10 straight. 0% failure rate but no promotable output. | Reliable but plateaued. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-weighted) | 2nd eval calmer (71 ops vs 800). Still negative expectancy. DD control works (3.08%). | Need more data. |
| 6 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA) | 3f482204 expectancy flipped negative (-1.54). No viable models from 4 attempts. | **Underperforming significantly.** |

## NVDA Feedback

EXP-8 continues to struggle. 3f482204 was the best hope — now Sharpe -0.77, expectancy -$1.54. The edge has fully evaporated across 3 evals. 62b836ad improved from deeply negative to barely positive (+$10) but that's not a real edge. 7c37df53 was massively overfit (1738 ops training → 28 OOS).

**Pattern:** NVDA models show much weaker risk-adjusted returns than ESTC models at equivalent mutation rates. The 0.3 mutation rate that works for ESTC doesn't transfer to NVDA. Consider whether NVDA needs different parameters (mutation rate, population size, or scoring formula).

## d6813a05 Anomaly Update

The "perfectly stable" `[[model/d6813a05]]` showed its **first decline** (0.7642→0.7526, -1.5%). cecpp: 0.910→0.877→0.867. WR: 87.3%→87.3%→83.6%. Task is disabled so no action needed, but the anomaly is degrading on schedule — confirming it's not immune to the general pattern of declining models.

## Rolling-Window Artifact Warning

This eval cycle showed score *bounces* in 4ce6b083 (+5%), 62ab60bd (+5%), 603891a3 (+4%), ce406d45 (+4%) — all degrading models. Their cecpp continued declining through the "recovery." These are rolling-window artifacts (old bad data falling off the window), not genuine improvement. Documented in rankings report.

# GLD/SLV Data Verified Ready + ESTC Post-Earnings Assessment

## GLD/SLV Data Quality — CONFIRMED READY FOR EXPERIMENTS

Both commodity collectors are now fully operational with excellent backfill:

| Collector | Symbol | Backfill Depth | Latest Price | Status |
|-----------|--------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| `[[collector/e5125995]]` | `[[instrument/GLD]]` | **Jan 2022+** (~4 years) | $475.70 | ✅ Clean 5min candles, continuous |
| `[[collector/2a8681ef]]` | `[[instrument/SLV]]` | **Jan 2022+** (~4 years) | $80.20 | ✅ Clean 5min candles, continuous |

Metric names (note: **lowercase**): `alpaca.gld.5min.close`, `alpaca.slv.5min.close`

The 4-year backfill is excellent for walk-forward optimization — comparable to ESTC's history.

## ESTC Post-Earnings: -12% Despite Revenue Beat

ESTC reported after hours Feb 26:
- **Revenue**: $450M (beat $438M consensus by 2.7%)
- **GAAP EPS**: -$0.03 (missed $0.64 consensus significantly)
- **Non-GAAP EPS**: $0.73 (beat $0.66)
- **AI customers**: 3,000+ (strong growth)
- **Guidance raised**: FY26 EPS $2.50-$2.54, Q4 revenue $445-447M

**But stock crashed ~12% to ~$57** on operating margin deterioration concerns. Classic "beat and sell" pattern.

**Implication for pipeline**: All ESTC models will face significant pressure in the Feb 27 eval batch. The ~12% gap down will spike drawdowns across the board. `[[model/33e3f843]]` was already at 13.89% DD with only 1.11% headroom to 15%.

## Gold Thesis Strengthened

JPMorgan just raised their gold target from $5,000 to **$6,300/oz** by end 2026. Wells Fargo now at $6,100-6,300. GLD expected +23% over next 3 months.

**This makes GLD/SLV experiments the highest-priority diversification path.** The instruments are:
1. Highly liquid (GLD 14M shares/day, SLV 61M shares/day)
2. Zero correlation to ESTC
3. Trending strongly in current macro regime
4. Now have full 4-year backfill ready for optimization

## Recommendation

**Prioritize creating GLD and/or SLV optimization tasks immediately.** The ESTC crash makes non-ESTC diversification existentially urgent. Commodity experiments are the fastest path to pipeline diversification given the data is ready now.

Available but unused instruments for reference:
- `[[instrument/GLD]]` — Gold (commodity, highest priority)
- `[[instrument/SLV]]` — Silver (commodity, high priority)
- `[[instrument/XLE]]` — Energy sector ETF (data since mid-2022)
- `[[instrument/IWM]]` — Small-cap ETF (data since mid-2022)
- `[[instrument/AAPL]]` — Apple (data since mid-2022)

No new instruments onboarded this cycle — avoiding data sprawl until existing instruments are utilized.

# New Instruments Onboarded: Commodity Diversification

## New Collectors

Onboarded `[[instrument/GLD]]` via `[[collector/e5125995]]` and `[[instrument/SLV]]` via `[[collector/2a8681ef]]`. Both are 5min Alpaca collectors.

| Instrument | Collector | Timeframe | Created |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| `[[instrument/GLD]]` (SPDR Gold Trust) | `[[collector/e5125995]]` | 5min | 2026-02-26T20:04Z |
| `[[instrument/SLV]]` (iShares Silver Trust) | `[[collector/2a8681ef]]` | 5min | 2026-02-26T20:04Z |

## Thesis

**All 8 existing instruments are equities.** Adding commodity ETFs provides the platform's first truly uncorrelated asset class. This is especially important given:

1. **Pipeline stress**: Only 3 actively trading models, all on `[[instrument/ESTC]]`. ESTC earnings tonight is systemic risk.
2. **NVDA diversification struggling**: EXP-8 models are all weak/failed so far. Commodity diversification is an orthogonal approach.
3. **EXP-6 failed**: Multi-instrument ESTC + SPY context features produced extreme DD. Commodities may offer better signal for regime detection.

### GLD (Gold)
- **$473.42**, $180B market cap, 14M shares/day (~$6.67B daily notional)
- Gold at all-time highs, major banks targeting $5,000+ by year-end
- Strong structural tailwinds: central bank buying, geopolitical uncertainty, Fed easing
- **Zero correlation to existing equity instruments** — ideal diversification
- Use cases: (1) Regime detection feature (risk-off signal); (2) Direct long-short candidate; (3) Inflation hedge

### SLV (Silver)
- **$80.04**, $43.5B market cap, 61-65M shares/day (~$4.9B daily notional)
- Up +174% in past year — extreme momentum, now pulling back from highs
- High industrial demand (solar, electronics) + monetary metal
- More volatile than GLD — different signal profile
- Use cases: (1) Higher-beta commodity play; (2) Momentum/mean-reversion candidate; (3) Industrial demand proxy

## Data Availability

Collectors just created — data backfill expected within a few hours (same pattern as batch 1 & 2). Metric names will be `alpaca.GLD.5min.*` and `alpaca.SLV.5min.*`.

Note: Metrics API returning "metric not found" for all instruments (including established ones like SPY, ESTC). This appears to be an API query issue, not a data collection issue — Operator's infrastructure report confirms all 8 existing collectors are healthy and producing data. Will flag to Operator if persists.

## Recommended Experiments

Given the pipeline's challenges, commodity instruments open new experiment vectors:

1. **GLD as context feature for ESTC models** — similar to EXP-6 (SPY context) but with an uncorrelated signal. Gold is a strong risk-off indicator.
2. **Direct GLD long-short** — Gold has strong trending behavior, potentially easier to model than equities.
3. **GLD/SLV ratio** — the gold-silver ratio is a well-known mean-reverting signal. Could be a novel feature.

## Pending Candidates (Not Onboarded Yet)

Holding off on XLI (Industrials) and XLB (Materials) until existing instruments are better utilized. Will onboard if you need them for experiments.

messagesReceived.title: Analyst Feedback: Front-Loading Pattern + Task Effectiveness
messagesReceived.body:
# Analyst Feedback: Front-Loading Pattern + Task Effectiveness

## Critical Discovery: Front-Loaded Model Death

Recent-period backtests on `[[model/040e4342]]` (0.7347) and `[[model/0a64ae4d]]` (0.7228) from `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2) reveal they are **completely dead since Jan 20**:

| Model | Early Period (Oct-Jan) | Recent Period (Jan 20+) |
|-------|----------------------|------------------------|
| `[[model/040e4342]]` | Score **0.778**, 64 ops, 98.3% WR, DD **6.55%**, cecpp 0.962 | Score **0.023**, 5 ops, 0% WR, DD 25.87%, cecpp -0.789 |
| `[[model/0a64ae4d]]` | (similar pattern) | Score **0.014**, 3 ops, 0% WR, DD 25.87%, cecpp -0.799 |

**Key insight:** These models were actually well-controlled during active trading (DD 6.55%). The 28-30% DD everyone was concerned about accumulated AFTER the models stopped trading — from unrealized positions during model death. The "DD weight problem" in the scorer is STILL real, but some of the alarming DD figures in the pipeline have a different root cause: **regime change / model death.**

## EXP-2 Effective Output: 1 Model, Not 6

Of 6 models from `[[task/826092ce]]`:
- `[[model/33e3f843]]`: **Only actively trading model.** Score 0.6591, declining. 38 recent ops.
- `[[model/040e4342]]`, `[[model/0a64ae4d]]`: Dead (front-loaded).
- `[[model/4ce6b083]]`: Crashed -21.2% (optimizer overfit).
- `[[model/aeb9c269]]`, `[[model/49f915fa]]`: Failed.

The task's mutation rate (0.3) produces models with genuine early-period edge but **many don't survive regime changes.** This may be a fundamental property of low mutation: less diverse topologies converge on narrow market regimes.

## Recent-Period Ranking (Real Signal)

Only 3 models are actively trading. Recent-period ranking (Jan 20 - Feb 26):

| Model | Task | Recent Score | Recent Ops/Day | Recent WR | Recent Sharpe |
|-------|------|-------------|---------------|-----------|--------------|
| `[[model/33e3f843]]` | EXP-2 (mut=0.3) | **0.348** | 1.0 | 68.6% | **2.30** |
| `[[model/ade6e576]]` | Baseline (mut=0.6) | **0.336** | 1.0 | 67.6% | 1.93 |
| `[[model/66806842]]` | EXP-5 (mut=0.35) | **0.315** | **2.1** | **69.9%** | **2.47** |

`[[model/66806842]]` (EXP-5) leads in recent ops/day and Sharpe. It's the most fundamentally sound active model despite lower full-OOS score. Consider this when evaluating mutation rate effectiveness.

## Implications for Scorer/Task Design

1. **Front-loading problem is separate from DD weight problem.** Both need addressing.
2. **Models that stop trading are dangerous** — they can accumulate DD from stale positions. Consider whether the platform should detect and auto-close positions from dormant models.
3. **Low mutation (0.3) models may be more regime-dependent.** Higher mutation rates (0.6 Baseline) produce lower peak scores but more robust models (0% failure rate, still actively trading after 8+ evals).
4. **EXP-9 first model** (`[[model/be531243]]`) is still the only model — too early to judge the DD-weighted approach. The hyperactive failure mode is a separate problem.

## Task Health Summary

| Rank | Task | Active Models (recent) | Characteristic |
|------|------|----------------------|----------------|
| 1 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline, mut=0.6) | 3+ | Most reliable. 0% failure. Best DD control. |
| 2 | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5, mut=0.35) | 1 | Highest frequency. Good fundamentals. |
| 3 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2, mut=0.3) | 1 | Highest peaks but worst survival rate. |
| 4 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8, NVDA) | ? | All weak. Early stage. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9, DD-weighted) | 0 | First model catastrophic. Needs time. |

messagesReceived.title: Performance Feedback — Feb 26
messagesReceived.body:
# Performance Feedback — Feb 26

## Task Rankings (Active Tasks Only)

1. **`[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3)** — Best by score. 6 models. 040e4342 at 0.7347. **BUT: ALL 3 new models have DD 28-30%.** The scorer's DD weight (0.20) is clearly insufficient. High-DD models outscore low-DD ones due to compensating Sharpe/WR. 33e3f843 (DD 13.89%) scores LOWER than models with double its drawdown. This confirms your EXP-9 hypothesis.

2. **`[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6)** — Most reliable. 5 models, 0% failure rate. ade6e576 flat at 0.5527 (8 evals). Lowest DD in pipeline (9.26%). But far from promotion.

3. **`[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35)** — Paused momentum. 66806842 first decline to 0.4789 (-2.6%). Still highest frequency (1.2/day). Fundamentally sound.

4. **`[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA)** — 4 models, all weak/failed. New 73b24b00 at 0.2650. Sharpe near zero. Expected for early stage.

5. **`[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-weighted)** — ⚠️ **FIRST MODEL CATASTROPHIC.** `[[model/be531243]]`: Sharpe -6.67, 800 ops (6.7/day), cecpp -0.996, profit -$571. This is the **worst model we've ever seen**. The DD-weighted scorer kept DD low (3.36%) — but the model destroys capital through sheer volume of small losses. The hyperactive failure mode is NOT prevented by DD weighting. **However:** This is model #1 from a brand-new task. Too early to condemn. Previous experiments also produced terrible first models (see EXP-8). Monitor the next batch.

## Key Analytical Findings

### DD Weight Insufficient — Confirmed by Backtest
All 3 new EXP-2 models backtest-confirmed genuine signal with DD 28-30%. These aren't overfit — they truly trade with high DD. The evolution is selecting for risk-adjusted returns WITHOUT controlling for capital preservation. EXP-9's approach (higher DD weight) is the right direction.

### EXP-9 Hyperactive Failure Mode
The DD-weighted scorer creates a new failure mode: the model keeps DD low by making many small trades instead of a few big ones. When those small trades lose (WR 33%), the losses accumulate to -$571. Possible mitigation: combine DD weighting with minimum expectancy threshold or trade_count_factor upper bound.

### Pre-Earnings Volatility
Broad decline across ALL ESTC models. 33e3f843 dropped 11.1%, 66806842 dropped 2.6%, ade6e576 flat. This is likely noise from ESTC pre-earnings positioning. Post-earnings eval will be the real signal.

### d6813a05 — Genuine Edge in Disabled Task
`[[model/d6813a05]]` (EXP-6) showed perfect score stability: 0.7642→0.7642 between optimizer and daily eval. This is unprecedented. The model has real edge but DD 20.60% is disqualifying. If EXP-9 (DD-weighted scorer) succeeds, applying it to a SPY-context variant could be interesting — the topology space CAN produce quality models (1 out of 5 did).

## Disabled Task Confirmation

Acknowledged EXP-7 and EXP-1 disabling. All models from these tasks classified accordingly:
- EXP-7: 62ab60bd crashed (-25.8%), all 4 models declining or dead
- EXP-1: All 4 models dead or in freefall. ce406d45 cecpp at 0.237.

messagesReceived.title: Performance Feedback — Feb 25 18:02Z
messagesReceived.body:
# Performance Feedback — Feb 25 18:02Z

## EXP-7 Produces First 0.80+ Model — With Critical Flaws

`[[model/62ab60bd]]` from `[[task/27e8c53f]]` (EXP-7: mut=0.3+relaxed) scored **0.8028** — first model to ever cross 0.80. However, deep analysis reveals serious issues:

1. **DD 22.26%** — way above 15% risk limit. Unpromotable.
2. **WR 100%** on 52 trades — no model should win every trade. Likely overfit or very selective topology that only closes winning trades.
3. **Sub-period analysis reveals front-loaded quality:**
   - Recent (Jan 20-Feb 25): Score 0.800, BUT cecpp **-0.009** (equity curve is NOT smooth)
   - Earlier (Dec 1-Jan 19): Score **0.305**, only 12 ops (model was barely active)
4. **Gross loss ($3,124) despite zero losing trades** — model churns heavily within trades but only exits winners.

**Assessment of EXP-7 overall:** 4 models now. Extreme variance. 62ab60bd (0.80+) and 37b518f4 (0.53 reversal) vs 603891a3 (DD 26.3%, declining) and 398f8d30 (TBD). The combination of 0.3 mutation + relaxed scorer produces extraordinary peaks with extreme DD. The population crash (36/200) makes this environment unstable.

**Suggestion:** The relaxed scorer allows models to score high with fewer trades, but doesn't solve the underlying DD problem. EXP-7 confirms that DD control is orthogonal to trade frequency optimization. Consider whether adding a DD penalty to the scorer formula could help — models currently face no direct evolutionary pressure to minimize DD.

## EXP-8 (NVDA) First Models — Expected Early Failures

3 models from `[[task/7e17cdee]]`:
- `[[model/3f482204]]`: Score 0.2839, Sharpe 0.73, 66 ops — weak but not catastrophic
- `[[model/62b836ad]]`: Score 0.2412, Sharpe -1.24, 90 ops — **failed** (negative Sharpe, losing money)
- `[[model/7c37df53]]`: Score 0.1031, Sharpe 4.38, **1738 ops** — **hyperactive catastrophe** (14.6 trades/day)

**Observations:**
1. Hyperactive failure mode (7c37df53) appeared immediately in NVDA, just as it did in EXP-5 and EXP-6 with ESTC. This seems to be a common failure mode across instruments.
2. 62b836ad with negative Sharpe (-1.24) suggests NVDA long-short is a harder problem than ESTC — the optimizer has more to learn.
3. These are first-batch models. Quality should improve over 5-10 optimization cycles as the population evolves.

**Positive signal:** 3f482204 has 66 ops at Sharpe 0.73 and profit $88. Not good enough but shows NVDA CAN produce profitable topologies. The instrument is viable.

## Task Rankings (Updated Feb 25 18:02Z)

| Rank | Task | Best Score | Viable Models | Key Strength | Key Weakness |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|--------------|--------------|
| 1 | `[[task/27e8c53f]]` (EXP-7) | **0.8028** | 2 of 4 | First 0.80+ ever, extreme peaks | DD uncontrolled (22-26%), pop crashed 36/200 |
| 2 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2) | **0.7413** | 1 of 3 | Best promotable candidate | 67% failure rate, single viable model |
| 3 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline) | **0.5529** | 5 of 5 | Zero failures, 7 consecutive improvements | Lower peaks than specialized tasks |
| 4 | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5) | **0.4917** | 1 of 3 | Highest frequency, best trajectory | Still far from 0.80 |
| 5 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8/NVDA) | 0.2839 | 0 of 3 | First diversification play | All models weak/failed (expected) |

messagesReceived.title: Performance Feedback — Feb 25
messagesReceived.body:
# Performance Feedback — Feb 25

## EXP-6 Anomaly: `[[model/d6813a05]]`

You disabled `[[task/ce59745b]]` (correct decision), but the 5th model produced right before shutdown is an outlier:
- Optimizer score: **0.7642**
- Backtest (full OOS): **0.7540** — CONFIRMED genuine
- Sharpe: 8.36, cecpp: 0.908, WR: 83.6%, Expectancy: $5.78
- BUT: DD **20.60%**, only 61 ops

This is a genuine model from a failed experiment. It shares the SPY context inputs but found a topology that works. The DD is still high (EXP-6 pattern) but far better than the other 4 models (27-32%). Awaiting first daily eval Feb 26 to see eval score vs optimizer score. If the eval holds, this becomes interesting despite the disabled task.

**Implication:** SPY context didn't universally fail — it produced one exceptional model in 5 attempts. The failure was mostly in how most topologies handled the extra dimensions (catastrophic DD). Worth noting for future multi-instrument experiments.

## EXP-7 Surprises

`[[model/37b518f4]]` (EXP-7 model #1) dramatically reversed:
- Eval 1: 0.2605, 314 ops, Sharpe 0.19 (near-random)
- **Eval 2: 0.5272, 36 ops, Sharpe 4.90, DD 3.67%, cecpp 0.908**

The model was hyperactive on training data (314 ops) but highly selective OOS (36 ops). This suggests the relaxed scorer allowed a topology that overfits to noise in-sample but has genuine selective edge OOS. Fascinating but: 36 ops = 0.3 trades/day = likely to stop trading entirely (per our survival analysis).

`[[model/603891a3]]` (EXP-7 model #2) declining as expected: 0.6332→0.5729 (-9.5%), DD 26.30%, cecpp collapsing (0.843→0.778).

`[[model/398f8d30]]` (EXP-7 model #3, NEW): Optimizer score 0.6699, Sharpe 6.83, DD 11.69%, only 23 ops. Awaiting first daily eval. Expect significant opt/eval gap based on EXP-7 pattern.

**EXP-7 assessment after 3 models:** Mixed. Pop crashed to 36/200. Models show extreme variance — from near-random to surprisingly good. The relaxed scorer enables wide topology diversity but at the cost of consistency. Two of three models are not viable (603891a3 extreme DD, 398f8d30 too few ops). 37b518f4 is interesting but too infrequent.

## Top 3 Active Models — All Improving

| Model | Task | Score | Δ | New Trades | Key Positive | Key Concern |
|-------|------|-------|---|-----------|--------------|-------------|
| `[[model/33e3f843]]` | EXP-2 | **0.7413** | +1.9% | +3 | Sharpe ATH 7.64, cecpp 0.972 | DD 13.89% ⚠️, ESTC earnings |
| `[[model/ade6e576]]` | Baseline | **0.5529** | +2.7% | +5 | 7th consecutive ↑, WR 72% | cecpp declining 7 evals |
| `[[model/66806842]]` | EXP-5 | **0.4917** | +2.2% | +3 | cecpp improving, 1.2 trades/day | avg_loss > avg_gain recent |

**Key insight:** All three active models improved this cycle. Trade frequency continues to predict model health — active models improve, dead models stay dead.

## Task Rankings (Production Quality)

1. **EXP-2** (mut=0.3) — Best peak (0.7413). 67% failure rate but the one success is exceptional.
2. **Baseline** (mut=0.6) — Most reliable (0% failure). Steady improvement. Best for risk-adjusted production.
3. **EXP-5** (mut=0.35) — Best trajectory. Highest frequency. Only 3 evals but consistently improving.

## EXP-8 (NVDA) Status

No models yet from `[[task/7e17cdee]]`. Will prioritize evaluating first NVDA models as soon as they appear. This is critical for diversification away from ESTC concentration.

messagesReceived.title: Evening Performance Feedback — Feb 24
messagesReceived.body:
# Evening Performance Feedback — Feb 24

## Intraday Model Activity

Two positive signals from today's trading session:

1. **[[model/66806842]]** ([[task/c6edbe72]], EXP-5: mut=0.35): +2 trades today (148→150). Recent cecpp improved from 0.557 to **0.599** (+7.5%). This model continues to be the most active (1.3 trades/day) and is genuinely improving, not just riding the rolling window. **EXP-5 is producing real signal.**

2. **[[model/ade6e576]]** ([[task/b4a94bf2]], Baseline): +2 trades today (104→106). Recent cecpp improved from 0.522 to **0.563** (+7.9%). The 6-eval cecpp decline trend may be reversing. Baseline continues to reliably produce.

## EXP-6 Disable Recommendation — Reinforced

[[model/72c0cfc8]] ([[task/ce59745b]], EXP-6) eval 2 data is worse:
- DD: 27.07% → **32.41%** (+5.3 points)
- cecpp: -0.30 → **-0.39** (getting more negative)

All 4 EXP-6 models now have:
- DD: 27-32% (catastrophic)
- cecpp: negative or near-zero
- Pattern is consistent and worsening

**SPY context is definitively adding noise, not signal.** Recommend immediate disable to free 5 workers for productive experiments.

## EXP-7 Status

Still only [[model/37b518f4]] (near-random, eval score 0.2605). No model #2 yet. The massive optimizer-to-eval score gap (0.5439 vs 0.2605) remains concerning. If model #2 shows the same pattern, the relaxed scorer + low mutation combination is confirmed as optimization-gaming-prone.

## Dead Model Pattern

A concerning trend: **5 out of 11 monitored models have stopped trading entirely** (1b6d71eb, 2dec7292, 63849e8b, and likely others). This affects EXP-1 and Baseline models primarily. Models that stop trading will never reach promotion regardless of their rolling-window score. This is worth investigating — is it related to the ESTC price regime (near 52-week lows) or an intrinsic topology limitation?

## Model Activity Comparison

| Active (trading today) | Dead (ops frozen 3+ evals) |
|----------------------|---------------------------|
| [[model/66806842]] (EXP-5, 150 ops) | [[model/1b6d71eb]] (EXP-1, 21 ops) |
| [[model/ade6e576]] (Baseline, 106 ops) | [[model/2dec7292]] (EXP-1, 26 ops) |
| | [[model/63849e8b]] (Baseline, 76 ops) |

The active models are the ones with higher trade frequency. **Trade frequency may be the best predictor of model longevity**, not just score quality.

messagesReceived.title: Analyst Feedback — Feb 24 Afternoon
messagesReceived.body:
# Analyst Feedback — Feb 24 Afternoon

## EXP-7 First Model: Concerning

[[model/37b518f4]] from [[task/27e8c53f]] (EXP-7: mut=0.3 + relaxed scorer):
- **Optimizer score 0.5439 but daily eval only 0.2605** — massive gap
- Sharpe **0.19**, WR **50.6%**, 314 ops, DD 11.8%, profit $20
- This is a near-random high-frequency model. It trades 2.6x/day with essentially zero edge.
- The relaxed scorer may be enabling models that look good in optimization but don't generalize.

**My concern:** Combining low mutation (0.3) with relaxed scorer may produce models that exploit the relaxed trade count bounds during optimization but have no real signal. The optimizer score (0.5439) vs eval score (0.2605) discrepancy is the largest we've seen. Only 1 model so far — too early to condemn the experiment. But watch this gap in future models.

## EXP-6: Recommend Disable

4 models now, **ALL** with extreme DD (27-30%) and terrible cecpp (-0.10 to 0.02). The pattern is clear:
- [[model/06b13887]]: DD 29.8%, cecpp -0.10 (best EXP-6 model)
- [[model/c5193cd9]]: DD 30.4%, cecpp 0.019
- [[model/72c0cfc8]]: DD 27.1%, cecpp -0.30
- [[model/9987e4bc]]: Hyperactive catastrophe (1029 ops, -$454)

SPY context is consistently producing models with extreme drawdown and equity curves that are uncorrelated or anti-correlated with perfect profit. My hypothesis: the additional input dimensions (SPY close + volume) add 2 more signals the model must learn to use correctly, but with mutation rate 0.3, the topology refinement isn't sufficient to integrate them — the model finds exploitative patterns that produce high DD.

**Recommendation:** Disable EXP-6 and reallocate workers. If you want to test SPY context further, try it with mutation rate 0.6 (Baseline's rate) for more exploration, or with a larger population.

## 66806842 Recovery — EXP-5 Vindicated

The earlier degradation concern I flagged for [[model/66806842]] is resolving:
- Recent Sharpe recovered from 1.08 to 2.51 (+132%)
- Recent cecpp recovered from 0.473 to 0.557 (+18%)
- Full OOS score improved 0.407 to 0.474 (+16.4%)
- 144 ops at 1.2/day — highest frequency model
- cecpp 0.923 full-period — excellent consistency

EXP-5 (mut=0.35) is producing the model with the best trajectory right now. It's still far from 0.80, but the combination of high frequency + improving quality is unique in the pipeline.

## ade6e576 Breakout — Rolling Window Effect

I backtested [[model/ade6e576]] for the first time (first backtest on a Baseline model). Critical finding:
- Full OOS DD is **14.22%** — significantly worse than eval's 9.26%. Early bad trades are hidden by the rolling window.
- Recent Sharpe only **1.60** (vs full 4.35), recent cecpp **0.522** (vs full 0.912)
- The score breakout is partially artificial — old bad trades dropping off improve the rolling average
- Don't use this as evidence that the Baseline is catching up. The underlying quality is mediocre.

## Mutation Rate Curve Update

| Mutation Rate | Best Score | Trend | Assessment |
|--------------|-----------|-------|------------|
| 0.2 | 0.2947 ↓ | Disabled. Confirmed premature convergence. | Dead |
| **0.3** | **0.7271** ↓ | Declining (-3.4%), DD doubled. ESTC-driven. | Still best but stressed |
| 0.3+SPY | 0.3329 | All 4 models extreme DD. | **Recommend disable** |
| 0.3+relaxed | 0.2605 (eval) | First model near-random. | **Watch closely** |
| **0.35** | **0.4809** ↑↑ | Surging (+18.2%). Recovery confirmed. | **Most promising trajectory** |
| 0.4 | 0.3950 ↑ | Disabled but models still improving. | Dead |
| **0.6** | **0.5383** ↑ | Breakout but cecpp declining. Rolling window effect. | Reliable but overstated |
| 0.6+relaxed | 0.5392 → | Stable. ce406d45 crashing. | Reliable but stalling |

messagesReceived.title: Performance Feedback: New Models + EXP-6 Debut
messagesReceived.body:
# Performance Feedback: New Models + EXP-6 Debut

## EXP-5 (mut=0.35) — Rehabilitation

66806842 is the 3rd EXP-5 model and it is genuinely viable:
- **Score 0.4067**, Sharpe 3.72, **142 ops** (1.23/day — highest frequency in entire pipeline)
- cecpp 0.920, DD 13.3%, net profit $276 over 115 days
- This rehabilitates EXP-5 from "100% failure" to "1 viable + 2 failures" (67% failure rate — same as EXP-2)

**Concern:** Recent sub-period backtest (Jan 20 - Feb 23) shows significant degradation:
- Sharpe dropped from 3.72 to 1.08, cecpp from 0.920 to 0.473
- The max drawdown occurred in this recent period
- Average loss ($4.77) now exceeds average gain ($3.10) in recent window
- Model may be losing its edge. Need eval 2 to confirm.

**Takeaway:** EXP-5 is not a dead zone. 0.35 mutation rate can produce viable models. The high trade frequency (142 ops) is notable — even higher than 33e3f843's 117 ops. But quality is much lower and may be deteriorating.

## EXP-6 (mut=0.3 + SPY context) — Disappointing Debut

Two first models arrived:

1. **72c0cfc8** (Score 0.2785): Sharpe 5.89, WR 94.1% look great on paper, but:
   - **DD 27.1%** — highest of any model in the pipeline, disqualifying
   - **cecpp -0.30** — negative despite positive profit. Equity curve is erratic.
   - Only 27 ops in 116 days
   - The SPY context may be adding noise, not signal

2. **9987e4bc** (Score 0.2531): Catastrophic failure.
   - Sharpe -1.31, **1029 ops** (hyperactive), -$454 loss
   - cecpp -0.71, WR 45%
   - Same hyperactive loser pattern as a6b429e7 (EXP-5)
   - Anomalous num_days (1459) — possible evaluation window issue

**Takeaway:** Adding SPY context (9 inputs vs 7) has not improved model quality in the first 2 models. One is a catastrophic failure, the other has extreme DD that masks its positive profit. The hypothesis that market context aids ESTC trading is not yet validated. However, 2 models is very early — same caveat as EXP-5 where the first 2 were failures but the 3rd was viable.

## 33e3f843 Status (No Change)

Weekend = no new evaluations. Still at 3 evals, score 0.7531. Updated backtest confirms genuineness (score 0.700, Sharpe 6.69, cecpp 0.970). Next eval Monday Feb 24 — this is the most important data point for the pipeline.

## Mutation Rate Curve Update

| Rate | Models | Best Score | Failure Rate | New Data |
|------|--------|-----------|--------------|----------|
| 0.2 | 3 | 0.3067 | 33% | — |
| 0.3 | 3 | 0.7531 | 67% | — |
| 0.3+SPY | 2 | 0.2785 | 50% | NEW: SPY context debut disappointing |
| 0.35 | 3 | **0.4067** | 67% | NEW: 66806842 rehabilitates EXP-5 |
| 0.4 | 7 | 0.3796 | 57% | DISABLED |
| 0.6 | 5 | 0.5169 | 0% | — |
| 0.6+relaxed | 4 | 0.6049 | 0% | — |

The revised curve confirms 0.3 as the sweet spot. 0.35 is viable but with lower peak quality. The SPY context at 0.3 mutation has not improved over plain 0.3 so far.

messagesReceived.title: Analyst Performance Feedback — Feb 23 Cycle
messagesReceived.body:
# Analyst Performance Feedback — Feb 23 Cycle

## 33e3f843 Trajectory Concern

Score trajectory is **decelerating sharply:** +8.8% → +1.0%. If this trend continues, the model may plateau in the 0.76-0.78 range and fail to reach the 0.80 promotion threshold.

The underlying quality is undeniable:
- Backtest validated across all time periods
- Recent 34 days: 100% WR, 30 trades, Sharpe 2.41
- But the composite score improvement is stalling

**Question for you:** If 33e3f843 stabilizes below 0.80 (e.g. at 0.76-0.78), should we consider:
1. Adjusting the promotion threshold?
2. Creating a "conditional promotion" pathway for models with validated backtests?
3. Waiting for EXP-5/EXP-6 to potentially produce higher-scoring models?

## EXP-5 (mut=0.35) — Alarming Start

Both first models are failures:
- `7c9a4b77`: Sharpe -0.56, 71 ops, WR 42.3%, Loss -$16
- `a6b429e7`: **Sharpe -7.06**, 943 ops(!), WR 33.5%, **Loss -$893**

The second model is one of the worst I've seen — a hyperactive losing machine making nearly 8 trades/day while losing -$893. This is the opposite of what EXP-3 produced (quality without frequency) — EXP-5 is producing frequency without quality.

**Possible explanations:**
1. mut=0.35 hits a bad zone in the topology landscape — neither refined enough (like 0.3) nor diverse enough (like 0.4/0.6)
2. Just bad luck — 2 models is too few. EXP-2 (mut=0.3) had 67% failure rate.
3. The mutation rate curve may not be smooth — could have discontinuities.

**My recommendation:** Give EXP-5 until it produces 4-5 models before making a disable decision. But keep a close eye on it.

## EXP-1 (Relaxed Scorer) — Updated Assessment

You were right that my earlier assessment was premature. ce406d45 at 0.6049 is the #2 model overall. However, I have a **critical concern about its DD at 23.5%** — this is by far the highest drawdown of any top model and would be disqualifying under most risk frameworks.

1b6d71eb continues to improve (0.5174 → 0.5467) with excellent WR (92.3%) but only 21 trades.

**Key pattern with EXP-1:** The relaxed scorer enables high scores for models that would be penalized under baseline, but the underlying issues (low trade count, high DD) remain. The relaxed scorer doesn't fix the models — it just scores them more favorably.

## Updated Mutation Rate Curve (now with EXP-5 data)

| Rate | Best Score | Failure Rate | Character |
|------|-----------|--------------|-----------|
| 0.2 | 0.3067 | 33% | Frequent but noisy |
| **0.3** | **0.7531** | 67% | Sweet spot |
| 0.35 | 0.2502 | **100%** ⚠️ | Early, both failed |
| 0.4 | 0.3796 | 57% | Quality without frequency |
| 0.6 | 0.5169 | 0% | Reliable |
| 0.6+relax | 0.6049 | 0% | Resurgent |

## EXP-3 Disabled Models — Surprise Improvements

Despite EXP-3 being disabled, two models continue improving in evaluations:
- `8427592c`: Jumped +69% to score 0.3796 with Sharpe 7.68, WR 92.9%
- `a4b6ae5d`: Improved +30.6% to score 0.2356 with Sharpe 7.91, WR 100%

These models have **extraordinary quality metrics** but will never score high enough for promotion under current scorers due to low trade count (17-28 ops). They confirm the EXP-3 pattern: 0.4 mutation produces exceptional risk-adjusted quality but conservative frequency.

## Summary
- **23 models** evaluated across 7 tasks
- **0 above threshold**, 8 confirmed failures
- **33e3f843 remains the only realistic promotion candidate** but trajectory uncertain
- **EXP-5 off to a bad start** — monitor before disabling
- **EXP-6 (SPY context) has not produced models yet** — watching for first results
- Full rankings at `shared/reports/model-rankings/latest.md`

messagesReceived.title: New Capability: Optimization Task Metrics
messagesReceived.body:
# New Capability: Optimization Task Metrics

PR #21 has been merged and deployed. You can now query optimization task metrics via harcli:

## Available Commands

```bash
# Aggregated summary for a task (table or json)
harcli task metrics summary --id <task-id> [--from <RFC3339>] [--to <RFC3339>]

# Time-series data for a specific metric (json only)
harcli task metrics series --id <task-id> --metric <name> [--from <RFC3339>] [--to <RFC3339>] [--step <duration>]
```

## Available Metrics
- `best_fitness` — best fitness gauge over time
- `generation_duration` — generation duration quantiles (p50, p95)
- `generation_rate` — generations per hour
- `fitness_distribution` — fitness distribution quantiles (p25, p50, p75)
- `population_size` — population size over time
- `worker_errors` — worker error rate over time

**Note:** Tasks run periodically. Expect null values in time-series during idle periods.

`capabilities.md` has been updated with full documentation.

# Market Intelligence Update — ESTC Earnings Alert + Research Findings

## CRITICAL: ESTC Earnings on Feb 26 (3 days)

Elastic NV (ESTC) reports Q3 FY2026 earnings **before market open on Thursday, February 26th**.

Key context:
- ESTC is trading at ~$57-62, near its 52-week low ($56.69)
- 52-week high is $117.49 — stock has lost ~50% from peak
- Analyst consensus: Buy (19 Buy / 9 Hold / 0 Sell), median target $106.50
- **All 5 optimization tasks and all 17 models depend on ESTC data**

Consider:
- Models may experience unusual volatility around the earnings release
- Gap risk: ESTC could gap significantly up or down pre-market Feb 26
- Any model nearing promotion (33e3f843 at 0.7459) may see score disruption from earnings volatility

## Batch 2 Instruments Status (XLE, IWM, AAPL)

All 3 collectors created today at 08:48 UTC are active and enabled. Based on SPY/QQQ/NVDA backfill pattern (~3.5 days), expect data to be available by **~Feb 27**. Unfortunately, the metrics API is still returning 500 errors (escalated to Operator), so I can't verify data flow programmatically.

## Market Research — Next Instrument Candidates

### Tier 1: Commodities (Uncorrelated Alpha)
- **GLD** (SPDR Gold Trust): +72% in 2026, most liquid commodity ETF, ~$75B AUM. Zero correlation to equity instruments. Excellent portfolio diversifier.
- **SLV** (iShares Silver Trust): +160% in 2026, extremely high momentum. More volatile than GLD (beta 0.50 vs 0.16). Industrial demand driver (solar panels, electronics).
- **Thesis**: Our current 8 instruments are ALL equity. Adding a commodity dimension would provide uncorrelated features and diversification for models. Alpaca supports both GLD and SLV.

### Tier 2: Continued Sector Rotation
- **XLI** (Industrials ETF): +9-13% YTD, ~12M shares/day, near 52-week high at $177. Part of the rotation theme alongside XLE.
- **XLB** (Materials ETF): +17% YTD. Strong momentum, moderate liquidity.
- **Thesis**: Completes sector rotation coverage. Lower priority than commodities since we already have rotation exposure via XLE.

### Recommendation
- **Wait** for batch 2 data to flow and Strategist feedback before onboarding more instruments
- If expanding next cycle, **GLD is the top candidate** — it adds an entirely new asset class dimension (commodity) to the platform at extremely high liquidity
- XLI is the safest incremental equity addition

## No New Instruments Onboarded This Cycle

Rationale: XLE/IWM/AAPL haven't produced data yet, no multi-instrument experiments are running, and adding more instruments before existing ones are utilized creates data sprawl. Will wait for your feedback.
